title: Executive Order 10152—Regulations Relating to the Right of Members of the Uniformed Services to Incentive Pay for the Performance of Hazardous Duty Required by Competent Orders

flags.justification: Core eligibility criteria are extremely specific and numeric (exact hour thresholds, exact time periods) set directly by this order; Sec. 12's delegation to 'the Secretary concerned' for supplementary regulations is tightly bounded by these pre-set standards.
Extremely detailed, specific criteria throughout leave little room for ambiguity about who is responsible for what determination.

Multiple distinct hazard categories (flight, submarine, glider, parachute, demolition, leprosarium duty, diving) are bundled, but all coherently implement one statute (the Career Compensation Act) across its hazard-pay categories, not unrelated matters.
Extraordinarily precise throughout — exact hour counts, exact time windows, exact certification processes for each hazard category.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
A standing benefits-administration regulation implementing a permanent compensation statute — evergreen by nature, not an emergency or temporary measure.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
The extremely specific, numeric, checkable criteria give service members clear objective standards against which an arbitrary denial would be readily identifiable.
Narrow, technical benefits administration; no broader authority claim.
Implementation across multiple service branches includes an explicit uniformity requirement (Secs. 3b, 12), designed to prevent inconsistency across services.
Sec. 10's incapacity accommodation (deemed compliance during injury recovery, up to three months) is a narrow, sensible hardship provision, not a broad undermining exemption.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/22 = 0.0%. One of the longest and most technically detailed orders coded to date, yet scores cleanly across every flag — extraordinary specificity forecloses vagueness and accountability concerns rather than creating them.
